architecture_decisions.recommendation: Create a modular Python script with clear separation of concerns: URL extraction, link validation, reporting, and GitHub integration. Use async/await for efficient concurrent link checking.
architecture_decisions.rationale: The repository contains thousands of links across many files; async HTTP requests will significantly reduce total execution time. Modular design allows for easier testing and maintenance.

architecture_decisions.recommendation: Store link checking results in structured format (JSON) alongside Markdown reports for programmatic access. Include metadata like timestamp, HTTP status, redirect chain, and validation duration.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Structured data enables historical analysis, trend tracking, and potential future dashboard visualization. JSON format is easily consumable by other tools.

architecture_decisions.recommendation: Implement robust error handling with retry logic, exponential backoff, and circuit breaker pattern for external HTTP requests. Log errors at appropriate levels for monitoring.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Link checking involves unreliable external networks; robust error handling prevents false positives and ensures the script completes even with intermittent failures.

architecture_decisions.recommendation: Integrate link checking into existing GitHub Actions workflow structure with scheduled weekly runs and manual triggers. Use issue creation API to report new broken links.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Leverages existing CI infrastructure and follows repository patterns. Scheduled runs ensure regular maintenance without manual intervention.

architecture_decisions.recommendation: Use YAML configuration file with sensible defaults for timeouts, retries, concurrent connections, and exclusion patterns. Allow environment variable overrides.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Centralized configuration simplifies maintenance and allows non-developers to adjust parameters. Environment variables support CI/CD integration.

architecture_decisions.recommendation: Generate human-readable Markdown reports with clear organization by language/category and severity. Include summary statistics and comparison with previous runs.
architecture_decisions.rationale: Markdown format is familiar to contributors and renders well on GitHub. Clear organization helps prioritize fixing efforts.

tickets.title: Create link checking script foundation
tickets.description: Implement core Python script structure with configuration loading, Markdown parsing, and URL extraction from books/, casts/, and courses/ directories. Create initial config.yaml with default settings.

tickets.title: Implement async HTTP link validation
tickets.description: Add asynchronous HTTP client with retry logic, timeout handling, and redirect following. Implement concurrent link checking with configurable limits.

tickets.title: Add reporting functionality
tickets.description: Implement report generation in both Markdown and JSON formats. Create reports/ directory structure and include summary statistics, broken links by file, and comparison data.

tickets.title: Create GitHub Actions workflow
tickets.description: Add scheduled workflow to run link checker weekly. Configure workflow to generate reports and upload them as artifacts. Integrate with existing CI patterns.

tickets.title: Implement automated issue creation
tickets.description: Add functionality to automatically create GitHub issues for files with new broken links. Include issue templates with broken link details and fix instructions.

tickets.title: Update documentation
tickets.description: Update CONTRIBUTING.md to document the link checking process, how to interpret reports, and guidelines for fixing broken links. Add section on automated monitoring.

tickets.title: Add comprehensive testing
tickets.description: Create unit tests for URL extraction, HTTP validation, and report generation. Add integration tests with mock HTTP responses. Ensure test coverage for edge cases.

tickets.title: Optimize performance and add monitoring
tickets.description: Implement performance optimizations including connection pooling, cache headers respect, and parallel processing improvements. Add logging and metrics collection.

technology_choices.rationale: Provides high-performance asynchronous HTTP client suitable for checking thousands of links concurrently. Well-maintained with good timeout and retry support.

technology_choices.choice: Python's built-in regex with custom parsing
technology_choices.rationale: Lightweight approach that avoids external dependencies. The markdown structure in this repository is consistent (links in list items), making regex parsing reliable.
Standard YAML library for Python. Provides human-readable configuration format that's already used in the repository (see scripts/rtl_ltr_linter_config.yml).
Standard testing framework with excellent async support. Consistent with Python community standards and provides rich fixture system.
Already used in the repository for multiple workflows. Provides seamless integration with GitHub issues and artifact storage.
